content: read reviews/45.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/45.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/45.md.

1→# Review: `deepmind/45.lean`
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 45`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/45](https://www.erdosproblems.com/45)`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (k-colouring of divisors, monochromatic subset with reciprocals summing to 1).
    19→- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[Cr03]` citation for Croot's paper in *Annals of Mathematics*.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos45 ... end Erdos45`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos45`).
    25→- [x] `open Finset BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `middleDivisors` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing it as the set of divisors of n strictly between 1 and n.
    30→- [x] `noncomputable` not needed — `middleDivisors` is computable (filtering with decidable predicates).
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
    32→- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase` (`middleDivisors`).
    33→- N/A: No `local notation` needed — no problem-specific notation introduced.
    34→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check for `middleDivisors`. *(Optional "consider" item — not a hard requirement, but a sanity-check test confirming e.g. `middleDivisors 12 = {2,3,4,6}` would add confidence.)*
    35→
    36→## 6. Theorem Statements
    37→
    38→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    39→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_45` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    40→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib snake_case conventions.
    41→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
    42→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    43→- [x] Docstring references the source paper (`[Cr03]`).
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The original problem is a direct assertion ("For every k-colouring… there is a monochromatic subset whose reciprocals sum to 1"), proved by Croot. This is **Case 3** — a direct assertion, no yes/no question or specific-value query.
    48→
    49→- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) — correct for Case 3.
    50→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∃ n`, etc.) are in the theorem body after the colon, not as theorem parameters.
    51→
    52→## 7. Category Attribute
    53→
    54→- [x] Theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
    55→- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid. The problem was proved by Croot (2003) but is not formally verified in Lean.
    56→- N/A: `research open` sorry-check does not apply (category is `research solved`).
    57→
    58→## 8. AMS Attribute
    59→
    60→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
    61→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
    62→- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: `5 11`. ✓
    63→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    64→- [x] Both `5` (Combinatorics) and `11` (Number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    65→
    66→## 9. Proof Bodies
    67→
    68→- [x] Proof uses `by` with `sorry` on the next line (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
    69→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    70→- N/A: Not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    71→- [x] No informal proof sketches included (appropriate — this is `research solved`, not `research open`, but no sketch is needed regardless).
    72→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    73→
    74→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    75→
    76→- N/A: No variants present. The file contains only the single main theorem.
    77→
    78→## 11. Code Quality
    79→
    80→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    81→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `⊆`, `∈`, `∧`, `∑`, `ℕ`, `ℚ`.
    82→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    83→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    84→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
    85→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    86→
    87→## 12. Build Verification
    88→
    89→- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. *(Requires build environment.)*
    90→- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified. *(Requires build environment.)*
    91→
    92→---
    93→
    94→## Summary
    95→
    96→**Status: PASS** (modulo build verification)
    97→
    98→The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace structure, definition docstring, theorem naming, category/AMS attributes, `answer()` usage (correctly omitted — Case 3 direct assertion), and code style all meet requirements. The only non-checked items are the optional `@[category test]` sanity check for `middleDivisors` (a "consider" recommendation, not mandatory) and build verification (requires running `lake build`).

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/45.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 45`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/45](https://www.erdosproblems.com/45)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (k-colouring of divisors, monochromatic subset with reciprocals summing to 1).
- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[Cr03]` citation for Croot's paper in *Annals of Mathematics*.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos45 ... end Erdos45`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos45`).
- [x] `open Finset BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `middleDivisors` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing it as the set of divisors of n strictly between 1 and n.
- [x] `noncomputable` not needed — `middleDivisors` is computable (filtering with decidable predicates).
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase` (`middleDivisors`).
- N/A: No `local notation` needed — no problem-specific notation introduced.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check for `middleDivisors`. *(Optional "consider" item — not a hard requirement, but a sanity-check test confirming e.g. `middleDivisors 12 = {2,3,4,6}` would add confidence.)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_45` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib snake_case conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper (`[Cr03]`).

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion ("For every k-colouring… there is a monochromatic subset whose reciprocals sum to 1"), proved by Croot. This is **Case 3** — a direct assertion, no yes/no question or specific-value query.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) — correct for Case 3.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∃ n`, etc.) are in the theorem body after the colon, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid. The problem was proved by Croot (2003) but is not formally verified in Lean.
- N/A: `research open` sorry-check does not apply (category is `research solved`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: `5 11`. ✓
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `5` (Combinatorics) and `11` (Number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses `by` with `sorry` on the next line (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- N/A: Not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (appropriate — this is `research solved`, not `research open`, but no sketch is needed regardless).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The file contains only the single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `⊆`, `∈`, `∧`, `∑`, `ℕ`, `ℚ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. *(Requires build environment.)*
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified. *(Requires build environment.)*

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace structure, definition docstring, theorem naming, category/AMS attributes, `answer()` usage (correctly omitted — Case 3 direct assertion), and code style all meet requirements. The only non-checked items are the optional `@[category test]` sanity check for `middleDivisors` (a "consider" recommendation, not mandatory) and build verification (requires running `lake build`).

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
